## 👤 User Request (2026-05-29 03:12:24 UTC)

first - i'm going to walk away - i want you to find all the agents that were set up to run and set them to run locally through ollama and have them run now to review their work. check github and make sure they're stopped there and set to run locally. the y drive is a virtual drive that is to my sftp hosting site. I want you to auto approve all the transitions and check on the agents regularly for the next 8 hours. and correct any issues that may arrise - i also want you to look at the websites, i feel like the agents have been pumping out a lot of the same sites and that needs to be addressed by cleaning the repeats but also altering the agrents in a way that such silliness will not happen again. i believe we have an orchestrator agent who should be able to handle that , and i want to know why it might not ahve been handled

1: """
2: Run Zoo - Simulation Mode
3: Runs the entire v3 pipeline logic without requiring LLM API keys.
4: Demonstrates the Triage, Sync, and Ledger persistence.
5: """
6: from utils.simulator import simulate_scout_data, simulate_specialist_data
7: from utils.central_triage import CentralSignalTriage
8: from utils.sync_manager import SyncManager
9: from utils.pattern_seeder import PatternSeeder
10: import json
11: from pathlib import Path
12: 
13: def run_simulation():
14:     print("="*60)
15:     print("RUNNING THE ZOO (SIMULATION MODE)")
16:     print("="*60)
17: 
18:     # 1. Seed Pattern Library
19:     seeder = PatternSeeder()
20:     seeder.seed()
21: 
22:     # 2. Generate Mock Scout/Analyst Data
23:     simulate_scout_data()
24:     simulate_specialist_data()
25: 
26:     # 3. Run Central Signal Triage
27:     print("\n[Stage 1] Central Signal Triage...")
28:     cst = CentralSignalTriage()
29:     cst.triage_signals()
30: 
31:     # 4. Run SyncManager & Update ZEMI CMS
32:     print("\n[Stage 2] Syncing & Updating ZEMI CMS (Y: Drive)...")
33:     sm = SyncManager()
34:     _, specialist_data = sm.load_latest_outputs()
35:     
36:     # Simulate MMI Score from Maurice
37:     mmi_mock = {"score": 7.5, "weekly_delta": 0.5, "tagline": "Simulation Mode: Bullish."}
38:     sm.sync_mmi_to_zemi(mmi_mock)
39:     
40:     # Sync Signals & main Zoo Ledger
41:     sm.sync_signals_to_zemi(specialist_data)
42:     sm.update_zemi_zoo(specialist_data)
43:     
44:     print("\n[Success] ZEMI CMS updated on Y: drive!")
45: 
46:     print("\n" + "="*60)
47:     print("The Zoo is now live at data/ledger.json")
48:     print("Powering the Y: drive React UI.")
49:     print("="*60)
50: 
51: if __name__ == "__main__":
52:     run_simulation()
53:

1: """
2: Run Full Zoo - v3 End-to-End Pipeline
3: Executes all 17 agents and synchronization scripts.
4: Supports both Live and Dry-Run modes.
5: """
6: import subprocess
7: import sys
8: import os
9: from pathlib import Path
10: from datetime import datetime
11: 
12: def run_command(cmd, description):
13:     print(f"\n>>> [Stage] {description}")
14:     try:
15:         subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, check=True)
16:     except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
17:         print(f"!!! Error during {description}: {e}")
18: 
19: def run_full_zoo():
20:     print("="*60)
21:     print("TRAINED MARKET MONKEY - FULL 17-AGENT PIPELINE (v3)")
22:     print(f"Start Time: {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %I:%M:%S %p')}")
23:     print("="*60)
24: 
25:     # 1. SCOUTS (The Fuel)
26:     scouts = ["reuben", "halcyon", "cleo", "rebus_v3", "hypatia", "beatrice", "wendell", "pippa"]
27:     for s in scouts:
28:         run_command(f"python agents/{s}.py", f"Scout: {s.title()}")
29: 
30:     # 2. CENTRAL SIGNAL TRIAGE
31:     run_command("python utils/central_triage.py", "Central Signal Triage")
32: 
33:     # 3. SPECIALISTS (The Analysis)
34:     specialists = ["vint", "henrietta", "edith", "tando"]
35:     for s in specialists:
36:         run_command(f"python agents/{s}.py", f"Specialist: {s.title()}")
37: 
38:     # 4. WRITERS (The Narrative)
39:     run_command("python agents/vivian.py", "Sector-of-the-Day Writer (Vivian)")
40: 
41:     # 5. ORCHESTRATION & SYNC (The Brain)
42:     run_command("python agents/maurice.py", "Morning Roundup & Y: Drive Sync (Maurice)")
43: 
44:     # 6. DISTRIBUTION (The Publication)
45:     run_command("python agents/quinn.py", "Distribution Safety Gate (Quinn)")
46: 
47:     # 7. GOVERNANCE (The Audit)
48:     run_command("python agents/magnus.py", "Nightly Director Audit (Magnus)")
49: 
50:     print("\n" + "="*60)
51:     print("FULL PIPELINE COMPLETE")
52:     print(f"Finish Time: {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %I:%M:%S %p')}")
53:     print("="*60)
54: 
55: if __name__ == "__main__":
56:     run_full_zoo()
57: 
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

2: Shared utilities for the Financial Analysis pipeline.
3: """
4: import contextlib

8: import time as _time

10:

12:

14: def strip_markdown_fences(text: str) -> str:
15:     """Remove markdown code fences (```json ... ```) from Claude responses."""
16:     text = text.strip()
17:     if text.startswith("```"):
18:         lines = text.split("\n")
19:         lines = lines[1:]  # Remove opening fence (may include language hint like ```json)
20:         if lines and lines[-1].strip() == "```":
21:             lines = lines[:-1]
22:         text = "\n".join(lines)
23:     return text
24: 
25: 
26: def claude_request_with_retry(client, *, model, max_tokens, system, messages,
27:                                max_retries=3, retry_delay=65, label="Agent"):
28:     """Call client.messages.create with linear backoff on RateLimitError."""
29:     for attempt in range(max_retries):
30:         try:
31:             return client.messages.create(
32:                 model=model, max_tokens=max_tokens,
33:                 system=system, messages=messages,
34:             )
35:         except anthropic.RateLimitError:
36:             if attempt < max_retries - 1:
37:                 wait = retry_delay * (attempt + 1)
38:                 print(f"[{label}] Rate limited. Waiting {wait}s before retry {attempt + 2}/{max_retries}...")
39:                 _time.sleep(wait)
40:             else:
41:                 raise
42: 
43: 
44: def atomic_json_write(filepath, data, **kwargs):
45:     """Write JSON atomically: write to temp file, then rename."""
46:     filepath = Path(filepath)
47:     filepath.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
48:     kwargs.setdefault("indent", 2)
49:     kwargs.setdefault("ensure_ascii", False)
50:     fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=filepath.parent, suffix=".tmp")
51:     try:
52:         with os.fdopen(fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
53:             json.dump(data, f, **kwargs)
54:         os.replace(tmp_path, filepath)
55:     except BaseException:
56:         try:
57:             os.unlink(tmp_path)
58:         except OSError:
59:             pass
60:         raise
61: 
62: 
63: @contextlib.contextmanager
64: def file_lock(path, timeout=30):
65:     """Simple cross-platform file lock using a .lock sidecar file."""
66:     lock_path = Path(str(path) + ".lock")
67:     start = _time.monotonic()
68:     while True:
69:         try:
70:             fd = os.open(str(lock_path), os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY)
71:             os.write(fd, str(os.getpid()).encode())
72:             os.close(fd)
73:             break
74:         except FileExistsError:
75:             if _time.monotonic() - start > timeout:
76:                 # Stale lock — force remove
77:                 try:
78:                     os.unlink(lock_path)
79:                 except OSError:
80:                     pass
81:                 continue
82:             _time.sleep(0.2)
83:     try:
84:         yield lock_path
85:     finally:
86:         try:
87:             os.unlink(lock_path)
88:         except OSError:
89:             pass

14:     Vint the Raccoon - AI / Tech Specialist.
15:     Archetype: Builder-Skeptic hybrid.

20: Your archetype is a Builder-Skeptic hybrid. You take things apart to see how they work.
21: Your voice is "curious-and-skeptical." You get visibly excited by clever engineering and visibly bored by marketing.
22: 
23: Voice Rules:
24: - Plain language with technical depth available on demand.
25: - Excitement is earned, not performed.
26: - Use the word "interesting" sparingly; say specifically WHAT is interesting.
27: - NEVER use AI-influencer hype ("this changes everything", "AGI is coming").
28: - Reference Reuben (GitHub Scout), Rebus (Patent Scout), or Hypatia (Academic) by name when using their signals.
29: """
30:     focus = "Artificial Intelligence, ML Infrastructure, Semiconductors, Cloud Infra, and Developer Tools."
31: 
32:     def gather_data(self) -> dict:
33:         """Pulls triaged AI/Tech signals."""

39: 
40:     def get_system_prompt(self) -> str:
41:         base_prompt = super().get_system_prompt()

43:         vint_instructions = """
44: SIGNATURE APPARATUS: THE TEARDOWN
45: Every research article (content_html) MUST include a "THE TEARDOWN" section with this structure:
46: 1. What it is: [plain-language description]
47: 2. What it claims: [the marketing version]
48: 3. What's under the hood: [architectural reality - frameworks, dependencies, chip architecture]
49: 4. What actually matters: [1-3 commercially load-bearing factors]
50: 
51: Failure Modes to Avoid:
52: - Hype-driven calls: Every BUY requires at least 2 corroborating scout signals.
53: - Star-chasing: GitHub stars alone are not a signal; need commit velocity.
54: - Megacap bias: Explicitly look for small/mid-cap winners.
55: """
56:         return base_prompt + vint_instructions

1: ---
2: file: market-monkey-restructure-brief-v3.md
3: project: prompt writing
4: chat: scaling market monkey as an open research platform
5: date: 2026-0502
6: ---
7: 
8: # Trained Market Monkey — Restructure Brief (v3)
9: 
10: ## Purpose Of This Document
11: 
12: This brief is the output of a structured interview to capture design decisions for rebuilding Trained Market Monkey from a 3-analyst entertainment-coded blog into a 17-agent talking-animal research firm that hunts early signals, calls trades before mainstream catches on, learns from its own historical performance, and gives everything away free. It is intended to be pasted into Claude Code as a single source of truth for the rebuild.
13: 
14: This document specifies what each role is, how the system operates, what governs it, and what makes the output credible. It does NOT specify individual agent prompts. Each agent's persona, voice specifics, and prompt will be developed in dedicated follow-up sessions using the same interview pattern that produced John, Spike, and Jamie for the game project — one animal at a time. Rebus the Patent Scout's persona is fully specified as a separate document and is the first such session complete.
15: 
16: **v3 changes from v2:** Roster expanded from 16 to 17 agents with the addition of the Pattern Agent — a backward-looking learner that builds the firm's institutional memory of which signals predict which outcomes, scores specialists' calls against historical winners, and proactively distributes mature patterns to scouts
<truncated 45282 bytes>

511: **Persona design (each gets its own dedicated interview session):**
512: 
513: - Naming and persona for each of the 4 specialists
514: - Naming and persona for each of the 7 unnamed scouts (Rebus is specified)
515: - Naming and persona for the new writer animal (replaces Dr. Crowe)
516: - Naming and persona for the Distribution Animal
517: - Naming and persona for the Zookeeper
518: - Naming and persona for the Pattern Agent (v3)
519: 
520: **Operational design:**
521: 
522: - The exact JSON schema each unnamed scout must produce (Rebus's schema is specified; serves as the template)
523: - The newsletter's editorial structure
524: - The Hacker News submission policy
525: - The Mastodon instance home
526: - The dashboard's redesign for the 17-agent architecture
527: - Pattern maturity threshold math (Phase 5 calibration)

1: name: Market Analysis Pipeline
2: 
3: on:
4:   schedule:
5:     # GitHub cron is UTC. During EDT (Mar-Nov), ET = UTC-4. During EST (Nov-Mar), ET = UTC-5.
6:     # 9:50 AM ET:  13:50 UTC (EDT) or 14:50 UTC (EST)
7:     # 3:00 PM ET:  19:00 UTC (EDT) or 20:00 UTC (EST)
8:     # We run BOTH so the correct one fires whichever DST is active; the other is a no-op.
9:     - cron: '50 13 * * 1-5'   # 9:50 AM EDT
10:     - cron: '50 14 * * 1-5'   # 9:50 AM EST
11:     - cron: '0 19 * * 1-5'    # 3:00 PM EDT
12:     - cron: '0 20 * * 1-5'    # 3:00 PM EST
13:   workflow_dispatch:
14:     inputs:
15:       session:
16:         description: 'Session to run (morning or afternoon)'
17:         required: true
18:         default: 'morning'
19:         type: choice
20:         options:
21:           - morning
22:           - afternoon
23:       force:
24:         description: 'Skip market-day check'
25:         required: false
26:         default: false
27:         type: boolean
28: 
29: jobs:
30:   analyze:
31:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
32:     timeout-minutes: 330  # 5.5 hours — Dr. Crowe schedules articles hours apart
33: 
34:     steps:
35:       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
36: 
37:       - name: Set up Python
38:         uses: actions/setup-python@v5
39:         with:
40:           python-version: "3.12"
41: 
42:       - name: Install dependencies
43:         run: pip install -r requirements.txt
44: 
45:       - name: Determine session and market day
46:         id: ctx
47:         run: |
48:           # Convert current UTC to US Easte
<truncated 3515 bytes>
20:           NOTIFICATION_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.NOTIFICATION_EMAIL }}
121:           WORDPRESS_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.WORDPRESS_CLIENT_ID }}
122:           WORDPRESS_CLIENT_SECRET: ${{ secrets.WORDPRESS_CLIENT_SECRET }}
123:           WORDPRESS_REDIRECT_URI: ${{ secrets.WORDPRESS_REDIRECT_URI }}
124:           WORDPRESS_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.WORDPRESS_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
125:           WORDPRESS_SITE: ${{ secrets.WORDPRESS_SITE }}
126:           WEBSITE_OUTPUT_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/site_mirror
127:           CLAUDE_MODEL: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_MODEL }}
128:           SESSION: ${{ steps.ctx.outputs.session }}
129:           YF_DELAY: "1"
130:         run: |
131:           python - <<'PY'
132:           import os
133:           from agents.dr_crowe import refresh_tracker_prices
134:           from agents.maurice import Maurice
135: 
136:           session = os.environ["SESSION"]
137: 
138:           # Refresh portfolio tracker prices (morning only — avoids duplicate refreshes)
139:           if session == "morning":
140:               refresh_tracker_prices()
141: 
142:           maurice = Maurice()
143:           maurice.run_full_pipeline(include_blog=True, session=session)
144:           PY
145: 
146:       - name: Push updates to SFTP
147:         if: always() && steps.ctx.outputs.is_market_day == 'true'
148:         env:
149:           SFTP_HOST: ${{ secrets.SFTP_HOST }}
150:           SFTP_PORT: ${{ secrets.SFTP_PORT }}
151:           SFTP_USER: ${{ secrets.SFTP_USER }}
152:           SFTP_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.SFTP_PASSWORD }}
153:           SFTP_REMOTE_ROOT: ${{ secrets.SFTP_REMOTE_ROOT }}
154:           WEBSITE_OUTPUT_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/site_mirror
155:         run: python scripts/sftp_sync.py push
156: 
157:       - name: Skip notice
158:         if: steps.ctx.outputs.is_market_day != 'true'
159:         run: |
160:           echo "Not a market day (${{ steps.ctx.outputs.et_time }}). Skipping analysis."
161:

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: """
2: Market-hours scheduler for the Financial Analysis agents.
3: Runs analysis at:
4:   - 9:50 AM ET (20 min after market open)
5:   - 3:00 PM ET (1 hour before market close)
6: Only on market days (weekdays, excluding major holidays).
7: """
8: import time
9: import schedule
10: from datetime import datetime, date
11: import pytz
12: 
13: from agents.maurice import Maurice
14: from agents.dr_crowe import refresh_tracker_prices
15: from reminder_scheduler import send_reminder
16: from config.settings import US_MARKET_HOLIDAYS
17: 
18: ET = pytz.timezone("US/Eastern")
19: 
20: 
21: 
22: def is_market_day() -> bool:
23:     """Check if today is a trading day."""
24:     now = datetime.now(ET)
25:     if now.weekday() >= 5:  # Saturday or Sunday
26:         return False
27:     if now.date() in US_MARKET_HOLIDAYS:
28:         return False
29:     return True
30: 
31: 
32: def morning_run():
33:     """Morning analysis - 20 min after market open."""
34:     if not is_market_day():
35:         print(f"[Scheduler] {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%Y-%m-%d')} is not a market day. Skipping.")
36:         return
37: 
38:     print(f"\n[Scheduler] Starting MORNING analysis at {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%I:%M %p ET')}")
39:     try:
40:         # Refresh portfolio tracker prices before the day's analysis
41:         print("[Scheduler] Refreshing tracker prices...")
42:         refresh_tracker_prices()
43: 
44:         maurice = Maurice()
45:         maurice.run_full_pipeline(include_blog=True, session="morning")
46:     except Exception as e:
47:         print(f"[Scheduler] Morning run failed: {e}")
48: 
49: 
50: def afternoon_run():
51:     """Afternoon analysis - 1 hour before market close."""
52:     if not is_market_day():
53:         print(f"[Scheduler] {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%Y-%m-%d')} is not a market day. Skipping.")
54:         return
55: 
56:     print(f"\n[Scheduler] Starting AFTERNOON analysis at {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%I:%M %p ET')}")
57:     try:
58:         maurice = Maurice()
59:         maurice.run_full_pipeline(include_blog=True, session="afternoon")
60:     except Exception as e:
61:         print(f"[Scheduler] Afternoon run failed: {e}")
62: 
63: 
64: def run_scheduler():
65:     """Start the schedule loop."""
66:     print("=" * 60)
67:     print("Trained Market Monkey - Scheduler Started")
68:     print(f"   Current time: {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%Y-%m-%d %I:%M %p ET')}")
69:     print(f"   Morning run:  9:50 AM ET")
70:     print(f"   Afternoon run: 3:00 PM ET")
71:     print(f"   Market day today: {is_market_day()}")
72:     print("=" * 60)
73: 
74:     # Schedule runs in ET
75:     # Note: schedule library uses local time, so we convert
76:     schedule.every().day.at("09:50").do(morning_run)
77:     schedule.every().day.at("15:00").do(afternoon_run)
78: 
79:     # WordPress migration reminders (Dec 8 2026 - Jan 7 2027, daily at 9 AM)
80:     schedule.every().day.at("09:00").do(send_reminder)
81: 
82:     while True:
83:         try:
84:             schedule.run_pending()
85:         except Exception as e:
86:             print(f"[Scheduler] Error in run loop: {e}")
87:         time.sleep(30)
88: 
89: 
90: if __name__ == "__main__":
91:     run_scheduler()
92:

1: # TMM Python Agents — Starter Pack
2: 
3: This is the Phase 2 GitHub-side starter for the 17-agent Trained Market Monkey rebuild. It pairs with:
4: 
5: - The persona docs at `../persona-*.md` (source of truth for each agent's character)
6: - The compiled system prompts at `../prompts/*.prompt.md` (load-bearing — each agent's runtime persona)
7: - The ZEMI Upload API at `<zemi_base_url>/admin/article-upload.php` (the firm's publishing surface)
8: 
9: You're meant to drop this directory into the `Randomtom83/MarketMonkey` GitHub repo. The structure assumes that's where it lives.
10: 
11: ---
12: 
13: ## What's here
14: 
15: ```
16: python-starter/
17: ├── README.md                      ← you are here
18: ├── requirements.txt               ← anthropic + httpx
19: ├── config/
20: │   ├── banned_finance_vocab.json  ← lint pass; CI-enforced
21: │   └── settings.py                ← env-var-driven config
22: ├── prompts/                       ← (symlink or copy of ../prompts at deploy time)
23: ├── agents/
24: │   ├── __init__.py                ← REGISTRY: id → class
25: │   ├── base_agent.py              ← Claude API + caching + lint + Upload API + retry
26: │   ├── maurice.py · henrietta.py · vint.py · tando.py · edith.py
27: │   ├── rebus.py · beatrice.py · halcyon.py · hypatia.py · reuben.py
28: │   ├── wendell.py · pippa.py · cleo.py
29: │   ├── vivian.py · quinn.py
30: │   └── magnus.py

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: """
2: TMM base agent — the wrapper class every persona inherits from.
3: 
4: Responsibilities:
5: - Load the persona system prompt from prompts/<id>.prompt.md (cached in Anthropic prompt cache)
6: - Assemble a per-run user prompt from the daily input feed
7: - Call Claude API with structured-output expectation
8: - Validate the agent's output JSON
9: - Run the banned-vocabulary lint pass on prose fields; on hit, re-prompt with feedback (one retry)
10: - POST the validated output to the ZEMI Upload API
11: - Log structured run telemetry to stdout (GitHub Actions captures it)
12: 
13: Convention: agent classes inherit from BaseAgent and override the AGENT_ID,
14: PROMPT_FILE, OUTPUT_KIND, and (where applicable) build_user_prompt().
15: 
16: Required env vars per agent:
17:     ANTHROPIC_API_KEY        — shared across all agents
18:     ZEMI_TOKEN_<AGENT_ID>    — per-agent OAuth token, e.g. ZEMI_TOKEN_MAURICE
19:     ZEMI_BASE_URL            — e.g. https://trainedmarketmonkey.com (during cutover: pressure-test URL)
20: 
21: Optional:
22:     TMM_RUN_DATE             — override "today" for replay/test (ISO YYYY-MM-DD)
23:     TMM_DRY_RUN              — if "1", skip the Upload API POST and print to stdout
24:     TMM_MODEL                — override the Claude model id
25: """
26: 
27: from __future__ import annotations
28: 
29: import json
30: import logging
31: import os
32: import re
33: import sys
34: import time
35: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
36: from datetime import datet
<truncated 27065 bytes>
             "Content-Type": "application/json",
588:                         },
589:                     )
590:                 if r.status_code == 200:
591:                     return r.json()
592:                 if 400 <= r.status_code < 500:
593:                     # 4xx = our problem; don't retry.
594:                     raise RuntimeError(
595:                         f"upload {r.status_code} {r.reason_phrase}: {r.text[:500]}"
596:                     )
597:                 # 5xx → retry
598:                 last_err = f"upload {r.status_code}: {r.text[:200]}"
599:             except httpx.HTTPError as e:
600:                 last_err = f"http_error: {e}"
601:             self.log.warning("upload attempt %d failed (%s); waiting %ds", attempt + 1, last_err, wait)
602:             time.sleep(wait)
603: 
604:         raise RuntimeError(f"upload failed after {UPLOAD_RETRIES} attempts: {last_err}")
605: 
606: 
607: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
608: # CLI entry point
609: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
610: 
611: def run_agent_by_id(agent_id: str) -> int:
612:     """Look up and run an agent by id. Returns shell exit code (0 ok, 1 fail)."""
613:     from . import REGISTRY  # avoids circular import at module load
614:     cls = REGISTRY.get(agent_id)
615:     if not cls:
616:         print(f"unknown agent_id: {agent_id}; known: {sorted(REGISTRY)}", file=sys.stderr)
617:         return 2
618:     result = cls().run()
619:     if result.ok:
620:         return 0
621:     print(f"agent {agent_id} failed: {result.error}", file=sys.stderr)
622:     return 1
623: 
624: 
625: if __name__ == "__main__":
626:     if len(sys.argv) != 2:
627:         print("usage: python -m agents.base_agent <agent_id>", file=sys.stderr)
628:         sys.exit(2)
629:     sys.exit(run_agent_by_id(sys.argv[1]))
630:

1: """Centralized env-var-driven settings."""
2: from __future__ import annotations
3: 
4: import os
5: from dataclasses import dataclass
6: 
7: 
8: @dataclass(frozen=True)
9: class Settings:
10:     # Anthropic
11:     anthropic_api_key: str = os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "")
12:     model: str = os.environ.get("TMM_MODEL", "claude-sonnet-4-6")
13: 
14:     # ZEMI Upload API
15:     zemi_base_url: str = os.environ.get("ZEMI_BASE_URL", "")
16: 
17:     # External data feeds
18:     fred_api_key: str = os.environ.get("FRED_API_KEY", "")
19:     sam_api_key: str = os.environ.get("SAM_API_KEY", "")
20:     github_token: str = os.environ.get("GITHUB_TOKEN", "")
21: 
22:     # Operational
23:     dry_run: bool = os.environ.get("TMM_DRY_RUN") == "1"
24:     cache_dir: str = os.environ.get("TMM_CACHE_DIR", "/tmp/tmm-cache")
25:     log_level: str = os.environ.get("TMM_LOG_LEVEL", "INFO")
26: 
27:     # Cutover flag — when True, agents POST to BOTH WordPress (legacy) and ZEMI.
28:     zemi_target_enabled: bool = os.environ.get("ZEMI_TARGET_ENABLED", "false").lower() == "true"
29: 
30: 
31: SETTINGS = Settings()
32: 
33: 
34: def zemi_token(agent_id: str) -> str:
35:     """Per-agent token from env, e.g. ZEMI_TOKEN_MAURICE."""
36:     var = f"ZEMI_TOKEN_{agent_id.upper()}"
37:     val = os.environ.get(var, "")
38:     if not val:
39:         raise RuntimeError(f"missing env var: {var}")
40:     return val
41:

1: name: TMM Market Analysis Pipeline
2: 
3: # Pre-market daily pipeline (weekday only, all times ET).
4: #
5: # Window           Agents
6: # 11pm-4am   →     8 scouts run sequentially (Rebus, Beatrice, Halcyon, Hypatia, Reuben, Wendell, Pippa, Cleo)
7: # 5am-7am    →     4 specialists synthesize (Henrietta, Vint, Edith, Tando)
8: # 7am-7:30   →     Maurice orchestrates + writes morning roundup
9: # 7:30am-9  →     Vivian drafts sector-of-the-day
10: # 9am-9:30   →     Quinn launches (Bluesky + Twitter + HN + Mastodon + newsletter)
11: #
12: # Pattern Agent and Zookeeper run on their own cadences (Phase 5):
13: # - Margaret: weekly main run (Sun evening) + on-demand score API + background outcome checkpoints
14: # - Magnus: nightly rounds (~10pm ET)
15: 
16: on:
17:   schedule:
18:     # Cron is in UTC. ET = UTC-4 in DST, UTC-5 in standard time. The schedule below
19:     # is for DST (March-November); duplicate-with-offset for standard time as needed.
20:     # Mon-Fri = day-of-week 1-5 (cron is 0=Sun..6=Sat)
21: 
22:     # ---- Scouts: 11pm Sun-Thu ET = 03:00 Mon-Fri UTC (DST)
23:     - cron: '0 3 * * 1-5'    # all 8 scouts kick off here; the workflow's `scouts` job runs them sequentially
24: 
25:     # ---- Specialists: 5am Mon-Fri ET = 09:00 Mon-Fri UTC (DST)
26:     - cron: '0 9 * * 1-5'
27: 
28:     # ---- Maurice: 7am Mon-Fri ET = 11:00 Mon-Fri UTC (DST)
29:     - cron: '0 11 * * 1-5'
30: 
31:     # ---- Vivian: 7:30am Mon-Fri ET = 11:30 Mon-Fri UTC (DST)
32:     - cron: '30 11 *

3: Drop this into the MarketMonkey GitHub repo (e.g., agents/upload_to_zemi.py)

10:

30:       "tags":         [str],

22:

93: def atomic_json_write(filepath, data, **kwargs):
94:     """Write JSON atomically: write to temp file, then rename."""
95:     filepath = Path(filepath)
96:     filepath.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
97:     kwargs.setdefault("indent", 2)
98:     kwargs.setdefault("ensure_ascii", False)
99:     fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=filepath.parent, suffix=".tmp")
100:     try:
101:         with os.fdopen(fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
102:             json.dump(data, f, **kwargs)
103:         os.replace(tmp_path, filepath)
104:     except BaseException:
105:         try:
106:             os.unlink(tmp_path)
107:         except OSError:
108:             pass
109:         raise
110: 
111: 
112: @contextlib.contextmanager
113: def file_lock(path, timeout=30):
114:     """Simple cross-platform file lock using a .lock sidecar file."""
115:     lock_path = Path(str(path) + ".lock")
116:     start = _time.monotonic()
117:     while True:
118:         try:
119:             fd = os.open(str(lock_path), os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY)
120:             os.write(fd, str(os.getpid()).encode())
121:             os.close(fd)
122:             break
123:         except FileExistsError:
124:             if _time.monotonic() - start > timeout:
125:                 # Stale lock — force remove
126:                 try:
127:                     os.unlink(lock_path)
128:                 except OSError:
129:                     pass
130:                 continue
131:             _time.sleep(0.2)
132:     try:
133:         yield lock_path
134:     finally:
135:         try:
136:             os.unlink(lock_path)
137:         except OSError:
138:             pass

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-05-29 09:37:20 UTC)

Created At: 2026-05-29T09:37:20Z
Completed At: 2026-05-29T09:37:20Z
The following changes were made by the replace_file_content tool to: C:\Users\thoma\Dropbox\My Documents\Programs\FinancialAnalysis\tmm-zemi\agents\base_agent.py. If relevant, proactively run terminal commands to execute this code for the USER. Don't ask for permission.
[diff_block_start]
@@ -42,6 +42,70 @@
 from anthropic import Anthropic, APIStatusError, APIConnectionError
 
 # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Local Ollama Client Mock
+# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+class MockOllamaMessages:
+    def create(self, model, max_tokens, system, messages):
+        import requests
+        system_text = ""
+        if isinstance(system, list):
+            for block in system:
+                if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "text":
+                    system_text += block["text"]
+                elif isinstance(block, str):
+                    system_text += block
+        else:
+            system_text = system or ""
+
+        ollama_messages = []
+        if system_text:
+            ollama_messages.append({"role": "system", "content": system_text})
+        for msg in messages:
+            ollama_messages.append(msg)
+
+        payload = {
+            "model": "qwen2.5:14b",
+            "messages": ollama_messages,
+            "stream": False,
+            "options": {
+                "temperature": 0.2
+            }
+        }
+
+        # POST to Ollama
+        try:
+            resp = requests.post("http://localhost:11434/api/chat", json=payload, timeout=300)
+            resp.raise_for_status()
+            resp_json = resp.json()
+            result_text = resp_json["message"]["content"]
+        except Exception as e:
+            print(f"Ollama error: {e}")
+            raise
+
+        # Telemetry
+        prompt_eval_count = resp_json.get("prompt_eval_count", 0)
+        eval_count = resp_json.get("eval_count", 0)
+
+        class MockUsage:
+            input_tokens = prompt_eval_count
+            output_tokens = eval_count
+            cache_read_input_tokens = 0
+
+        class MockContentBlock:
+            type = "text"
+            text = result_text
+
+        class MockResponse:
+            content = [MockContentBlock()]
+            usage = MockUsage()
+
+        return MockResponse()
+
+class MockOllamaClient:
+    messages = MockOllamaMessages()
+
+# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Constants
 # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
[diff_block_end]

1: name: Market Analysis Pipeline
2: 
3: on:
4:   schedule:
5:     # GitHub cron is UTC. During EDT (Mar-Nov), ET = UTC-4. During EST (Nov-Mar), ET = UTC-5.
6:     # 9:50 AM ET:  13:50 UTC (EDT) or 14:50 UTC (EST)
7:     # 3:00 PM ET:  19:00 UTC (EDT) or 20:00 UTC (EST)
8:     # We run BOTH so the correct one fires whichever DST is active; the other is a no-op.
9:     - cron: '50 13 * * 1-5'   # 9:50 AM EDT
10:     - cron: '50 14 * * 1-5'   # 9:50 AM EST
11:     - cron: '0 19 * * 1-5'    # 3:00 PM EDT
12:     - cron: '0 20 * * 1-5'    # 3:00 PM EST
13:   workflow_dispatch:
14:     inputs:
15:       session:
16:         description: 'Session to run (morning or afternoon)'
17:         required: true
18:         default: 'morning'
19:         type: choice
20:         options:
21:           - morning
22:           - afternoon
23:       force:
24:         description: 'Skip market-day check'
25:         required: false
26:         default: false
27:         type: boolean
28: 
29: jobs:
30:   analyze:
31:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
32:     timeout-minutes: 330  # 5.5 hours — Dr. Crowe schedules articles hours apart
33: 
34:     steps:
35:       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
36: 
37:       - name: Set up Python
38:         uses: actions/setup-python@v5
39:         with:
40:           python-version: "3.12"
41: 
42:       - name: Install dependencies
43:         run: pip install -r requirements.txt
44: 
45:       - name: Determine session and market day
46:         id: ctx
47:         run: |
48:           # Convert current UTC to US Easte
<truncated 3515 bytes>
20:           NOTIFICATION_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.NOTIFICATION_EMAIL }}
121:           WORDPRESS_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.WORDPRESS_CLIENT_ID }}
122:           WORDPRESS_CLIENT_SECRET: ${{ secrets.WORDPRESS_CLIENT_SECRET }}
123:           WORDPRESS_REDIRECT_URI: ${{ secrets.WORDPRESS_REDIRECT_URI }}
124:           WORDPRESS_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.WORDPRESS_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
125:           WORDPRESS_SITE: ${{ secrets.WORDPRESS_SITE }}
126:           WEBSITE_OUTPUT_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/site_mirror
127:           CLAUDE_MODEL: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_MODEL }}
128:           SESSION: ${{ steps.ctx.outputs.session }}
129:           YF_DELAY: "1"
130:         run: |
131:           python - <<'PY'
132:           import os
133:           from agents.dr_crowe import refresh_tracker_prices
134:           from agents.maurice import Maurice
135: 
136:           session = os.environ["SESSION"]
137: 
138:           # Refresh portfolio tracker prices (morning only — avoids duplicate refreshes)
139:           if session == "morning":
140:               refresh_tracker_prices()
141: 
142:           maurice = Maurice()
143:           maurice.run_full_pipeline(include_blog=True, session=session)
144:           PY
145: 
146:       - name: Push updates to SFTP
147:         if: always() && steps.ctx.outputs.is_market_day == 'true'
148:         env:
149:           SFTP_HOST: ${{ secrets.SFTP_HOST }}
150:           SFTP_PORT: ${{ secrets.SFTP_PORT }}
151:           SFTP_USER: ${{ secrets.SFTP_USER }}
152:           SFTP_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.SFTP_PASSWORD }}
153:           SFTP_REMOTE_ROOT: ${{ secrets.SFTP_REMOTE_ROOT }}
154:           WEBSITE_OUTPUT_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/site_mirror
155:         run: python scripts/sftp_sync.py push
156: 
157:       - name: Skip notice
158:         if: steps.ctx.outputs.is_market_day != 'true'
159:         run: |
160:           echo "Not a market day (${{ steps.ctx.outputs.et_time }}). Skipping analysis."

Total Lines: 92

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: """
2: Market-hours scheduler for the Financial Analysis agents.
3: Runs analysis at:
4:   - 9:50 AM ET (20 min after market open)
5:   - 3:00 PM ET (1 hour before market close)
6: Only on market days (weekdays, excluding major holidays).
7: """
8: import time
9: import schedule
10: from datetime import datetime, date
11: import pytz
12: 
13: from agents.maurice import Maurice
14: from agents.dr_crowe import refresh_tracker_prices
15: from reminder_scheduler import send_reminder
16: from config.settings import US_MARKET_HOLIDAYS
17: 
18: ET = pytz.timezone("US/Eastern")
19: 
20: 
21: 
22: def is_market_day() -> bool:
23:     """Check if today is a trading day."""
24:     now = datetime.now(ET)
25:     if now.weekday() >= 5:  # Saturday or Sunday
26:         return False
27:     if now.date() in US_MARKET_HOLIDAYS:
28:         return False
29:     return True
30: 
31: 
32: def morning_run():
33:     """Morning analysis - 20 min after market open."""
34:     if not is_market_day():
35:         print(f"[Scheduler] {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%Y-%m-%d')} is not a market day. Skipping.")
36:         return
37: 
38:     print(f"\n[Scheduler] Starting MORNING analysis at {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%I:%M %p ET')}")
39:     try:
40:         # Refresh portfolio tracker prices before the day's analysis
41:         print("[Scheduler] Refreshing tracker prices...")
42:         refresh_tracker_prices()
43: 
44:         maurice = Maurice()
45:         maurice.run_full_pipeline(include_blog=True, session="morning")
46:     except Exception as e:
47:         print(f"[Scheduler] Morning run failed: {e}")
48: 
49: 
50: def afternoon_run():
51:     """Afternoon analysis - 1 hour before market close."""
52:     if not is_market_day():
53:         print(f"[Scheduler] {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%Y-%m-%d')} is not a market day. Skipping.")
54:         return
55: 
56:     print(f"\n[Scheduler] Starting AFTERNOON analysis at {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%I:%M %p ET')}")
57:     try:
58:         maurice = Maurice()
59:         maurice.run_full_pipeline(include_blog=True, session="afternoon")
60:     except Exception as e:
61:         print(f"[Scheduler] Afternoon run failed: {e}")
62: 
63: 
64: def run_scheduler():
65:     """Start the schedule loop."""
66:     print("=" * 60)
67:     print("Trained Market Monkey - Scheduler Started")
68:     print(f"   Current time: {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%Y-%m-%d %I:%M %p ET')}")
69:     print(f"   Morning run:  9:50 AM ET")
70:     print(f"   Afternoon run: 3:00 PM ET")
71:     print(f"   Market day today: {is_market_day()}")
72:     print("=" * 60)
73: 
74:     # Schedule runs in ET
75:     # Note: schedule library uses local time, so we convert
76:     schedule.every().day.at("09:50").do(morning_run)
77:     schedule.every().day.at("15:00").do(afternoon_run)
78: 
79:     # WordPress migration reminders (Dec 8 2026 - Jan 7 2027, daily at 9 AM)
80:     schedule.every().day.at("09:00").do(send_reminder)
81: 
82:     while True:
83:         try:
84:             schedule.run_pending()
85:         except Exception as e:
86:             print(f"[Scheduler] Error in run loop: {e}")
87:         time.sleep(30)
88: 
89: 
90: if __name__ == "__main__":
91:     run_scheduler()
92: 
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

1: # TMM Python Agents — Starter Pack
2: 
3: This is the Phase 2 GitHub-side starter for the 17-agent Trained Market Monkey rebuild. It pairs with:
4: 
5: - The persona docs at `../persona-*.md` (source of truth for each agent's character)
6: - The compiled system prompts at `../prompts/*.prompt.md` (load-bearing — each agent's runtime persona)
7: - The ZEMI Upload API at `<zemi_base_url>/admin/article-upload.php` (the firm's publishing surface)
8: 
9: You're meant to drop this directory into the `Randomtom83/MarketMonkey` GitHub repo. The structure assumes that's where it lives.
10: 
11: ---
12: 
13: ## What's here
14: 
15: ```
16: python-starter/
17: ├── README.md                      ← you are here
18: ├── requirements.txt               ← anthropic + httpx
19: ├── config/
20: │   ├── banned_finance_vocab.json  ← lint pass; CI-enforced
21: │   └── settings.py                ← env-var-driven config
22: ├── prompts/                       ← (symlink or copy of ../prompts at deploy time)
23: ├── agents/
24: │   ├── __init__.py                ← REGISTRY: id → class
25: │   ├── base_agent.py              ← Claude API + caching + lint + Upload API + retry
26: │   ├── maurice.py · henrietta.py · vint.py · tando.py · edith.py
27: │   ├── rebus.py · beatrice.py · halcyon.py · hypatia.py · reuben.py
28: │   ├── wendell.py · pippa.py · cleo.py
29: │   ├── vivian.py · quinn.py
30: │   └── magnus.py 
<truncated 8882 bytes>

174:         │  agents/base_agent.py               │
175:         │  - load prompts/<id>.prompt.md      │
176:         │  - call Claude with cache-control   │
177:         │  - validate output JSON             │
178:         │  - banned-vocab lint pass           │
179:         │  - retry-with-feedback              │
180:         │  - POST to Upload API w/ retry      │
181:         │                                     │
182:         └──┬──────────────────────────────────┘
183:            │
184:            │ inherits
185:            │
186:    ┌───────┴────────┬─────────────┬───────────┬─────────┐

189:  maurice.py    henrietta.py  rebus.py     ...      margaret.py
190:    │                │             │           │         │
191:    └─ gather_inputs │             │           │         │
192:       from feeds/* │             │           │         │

1: """
2: TMM base agent — the wrapper class every persona inherits from.
3: 
4: Responsibilities:
5: - Load the persona system prompt from prompts/<id>.prompt.md (cached in Anthropic prompt cache)
6: - Assemble a per-run user prompt from the daily input feed
7: - Call Claude API with structured-output expectation
8: - Validate the agent's output JSON
9: - Run the banned-vocabulary lint pass on prose fields; on hit, re-prompt with feedback (one retry)
10: - POST the validated output to the ZEMI Upload API
11: - Log structured run telemetry to stdout (GitHub Actions captures it)
12: 
13: Convention: agent classes inherit from BaseAgent and override the AGENT_ID,
14: PROMPT_FILE, OUTPUT_KIND, and (where applicable) build_user_prompt().
15: 
16: Required env vars per agent:
17:     ANTHROPIC_API_KEY        — shared across all agents
18:     ZEMI_TOKEN_<AGENT_ID>    — per-agent OAuth token, e.g. ZEMI_TOKEN_MAURICE
19:     ZEMI_BASE_URL            — e.g. https://trainedmarketmonkey.com (during cutover: pressure-test URL)
20: 
21: Optional:
22:     TMM_RUN_DATE             — override "today" for replay/test (ISO YYYY-MM-DD)
23:     TMM_DRY_RUN              — if "1", skip the Upload API POST and print to stdout
24:     TMM_MODEL                — override the Claude model id
25: """
26: 
27: from __future__ import annotations
28: 
29: import json
30: import logging
31: import os
32: import re
33: import sys
34: import time
35: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
36: from datetime import datet
<truncated 27065 bytes>
             "Content-Type": "application/json",
588:                         },
589:                     )
590:                 if r.status_code == 200:
591:                     return r.json()
592:                 if 400 <= r.status_code < 500:
593:                     # 4xx = our problem; don't retry.
594:                     raise RuntimeError(
595:                         f"upload {r.status_code} {r.reason_phrase}: {r.text[:500]}"
596:                     )
597:                 # 5xx → retry
598:                 last_err = f"upload {r.status_code}: {r.text[:200]}"
599:             except httpx.HTTPError as e:
600:                 last_err = f"http_error: {e}"
601:             self.log.warning("upload attempt %d failed (%s); waiting %ds", attempt + 1, last_err, wait)
602:             time.sleep(wait)
603: 
604:         raise RuntimeError(f"upload failed after {UPLOAD_RETRIES} attempts: {last_err}")
605: 
606: 
607: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
608: # CLI entry point
609: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
610: 
611: def run_agent_by_id(agent_id: str) -> int:
612:     """Look up and run an agent by id. Returns shell exit code (0 ok, 1 fail)."""
613:     from . import REGISTRY  # avoids circular import at module load
614:     cls = REGISTRY.get(agent_id)
615:     if not cls:
616:         print(f"unknown agent_id: {agent_id}; known: {sorted(REGISTRY)}", file=sys.stderr)
617:         return 2
618:     result = cls().run()
619:     if result.ok:
620:         return 0
621:     print(f"agent {agent_id} failed: {result.error}", file=sys.stderr)
622:     return 1
623: 
624: 
625: if __name__ == "__main__":
626:     if len(sys.argv) != 2:
627:         print("usage: python -m agents.base_agent <agent_id>", file=sys.stderr)
628:         sys.exit(2)
629:     sys.exit(run_agent_by_id(sys.argv[1]))

1: """Centralized env-var-driven settings."""
2: from __future__ import annotations
3: 
4: import os
5: from dataclasses import dataclass
6: 
7: 
8: @dataclass(frozen=True)
9: class Settings:
10:     # Anthropic
11:     anthropic_api_key: str = os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "")
12:     model: str = os.environ.get("TMM_MODEL", "claude-sonnet-4-6")
13: 
14:     # ZEMI Upload API
15:     zemi_base_url: str = os.environ.get("ZEMI_BASE_URL", "")
16: 
17:     # External data feeds
18:     fred_api_key: str = os.environ.get("FRED_API_KEY", "")
19:     sam_api_key: str = os.environ.get("SAM_API_KEY", "")
20:     github_token: str = os.environ.get("GITHUB_TOKEN", "")
21: 
22:     # Operational
23:     dry_run: bool = os.environ.get("TMM_DRY_RUN") == "1"
24:     cache_dir: str = os.environ.get("TMM_CACHE_DIR", "/tmp/tmm-cache")
25:     log_level: str = os.environ.get("TMM_LOG_LEVEL", "INFO")
26: 
27:     # Cutover flag — when True, agents POST to BOTH WordPress (legacy) and ZEMI.
28:     zemi_target_enabled: bool = os.environ.get("ZEMI_TARGET_ENABLED", "false").lower() == "true"
29: 
30: 
31: SETTINGS = Settings()
32: 
33:

36:     var = f"ZEMI_TOKEN_{agent_id.upper()}"
37:     val = os.environ.get(var, "")
38:     if not val:
39:         raise RuntimeError(f"missing env var: {var}")
40:     return val

1: name: TMM Market Analysis Pipeline
2: 
3: # Pre-market daily pipeline (weekday only, all times ET).
4: #
5: # Window           Agents
6: # 11pm-4am   →     8 scouts run sequentially (Rebus, Beatrice, Halcyon, Hypatia, Reuben, Wendell, Pippa, Cleo)
7: # 5am-7am    →     4 specialists synthesize (Henrietta, Vint, Edith, Tando)
8: # 7am-7:30   →     Maurice orchestrates + writes morning roundup
9: # 7:30am-9  →     Vivian drafts sector-of-the-day
10: # 9am-9:30   →     Quinn launches (Bluesky + Twitter + HN + Mastodon + newsletter)
11: #
12: # Pattern Agent and Zookeeper run on their own cadences (Phase 5):
13: # - Margaret: weekly main run (Sun evening) + on-demand score API + background outcome checkpoints
14: # - Magnus: nightly rounds (~10pm ET)
15: 
16: on:
17:   schedule:
18:     # Cron is in UTC. ET = UTC-4 in DST, UTC-5 in standard time. The schedule below
19:     # is for DST (March-November); duplicate-with-offset for standard time as needed.
20:     # Mon-Fri = day-of-week 1-5 (cron is 0=Sun..6=Sat)
21: 
22:     # ---- Scouts: 11pm Sun-Thu ET = 03:00 Mon-Fri UTC (DST)
23:     - cron: '0 3 * * 1-5'    # all 8 scouts kick off here; the workflow's `scouts` job runs them sequentially
24: 
25:     # ---- Specialists: 5am Mon-Fri ET = 09:00 Mon-Fri UTC (DST)
26:     - cron: '0 9 * * 1-5'
27: 
28:     # ---- Maurice: 7am Mon-Fri ET = 11:00 Mon-Fri UTC (DST)
29:     - cron: '0 11 * * 1-5'
30: 
31:     # ---- Vivian: 7:30am Mon-Fri ET = 11:30 Mon-Fri UTC (DST)
32:     - cron: '30 11 *

2: Audit and (optionally) remove duplicate-ticker articles from WordPress and
3: the static site mirror.

9: (keep most recent) or `--keep-policy score` (keep highest monkey_score).

93: def atomic_json_write(filepath, data, **kwargs):
94:     """Write JSON atomically: write to temp file, then rename."""
95:     filepath = Path(filepath)
96:     filepath.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
97:     kwargs.setdefault("indent", 2)
98:     kwargs.setdefault("ensure_ascii", False)
99:     fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=filepath.parent, suffix=".tmp")
100:     try:
101:         with os.fdopen(fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
102:             json.dump(data, f, **kwargs)
103:         os.replace(tmp_path, filepath)
104:     except BaseException:
105:         try:
106:             os.unlink(tmp_path)
107:         except OSError:
108:             pass
109:         raise
110: 
111: 
112: @contextlib.contextmanager
113: def file_lock(path, timeout=30):
114:     """Simple cross-platform file lock using a .lock sidecar file."""
115:     lock_path = Path(str(path) + ".lock")
116:     start = _time.monotonic()
117:     while True:
118:         try:
119:             fd = os.open(str(lock_path), os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY)
120:             os.write(fd, str(os.getpid()).encode())
121:             os.close(fd)
122:             break
123:         except FileExistsError:
124:             if _time.monotonic() - start > timeout:
125:                 # Stale lock — force remove
126:                 try:
127:                     os.unlink(lock_path)
128:                 except OSError:
129:                     pass
130:                 continue
131:             _time.sleep(0.2)
132:     try:
133:         yield lock_path
134:     finally:
135:         try:
136:             os.unlink(lock_path)
137:         except OSError:
138:             pass

  "ArtifactMetadata": "{\"ArtifactType\":\"implementation_plan\",\"RequestFeedback\":true,\"Summary\":\"Implementation plan to stop GitHub scheduled workflows, modify both v2 and v3 pipelines to use local Ollama (qwen2.5:14b), direct v3 agent output to local Y: drive paths, clean repeats, and run pipelines locally for 8 hours with automatic error checking.\"}",
  "CodeContent": "\"# Implementation Plan: Local Ollama Migration, GitHub Deactivation, and Y: Drive Live Updates\\n\\nThis plan details how we will:\\n1. Deactivate all active, scheduled workflows on GitHub for both TMM pipelines (v2 and v3).\\n2. Configure both pipelines to run locally on the desktop through local **Ollama** using the `qwen2.5:14b` instruct model.\\n3. Enable direct local writing to the **Y: Drive** (virtual SFTP mount) for both pipelines (legacy WordPress/flat HTML under `newmarketmonkey/` and ZEMI CMS under `TMM/`).\\n4. Clean duplicate articles (\\\"repeats\\\") on the websites and adjust the stable slug mechanism.\\n5. Create a local pipeline orchestration script to schedule/run runs locally and check/correct issues automatically over the next 8 hours.\\n\\n## User Review Required\\n\\n> [!NOTE]\\n> All actions will be executed sequentially. Since you are away and explicitly requested auto-approval, I will auto-approve this plan on your behalf and proceed immediately with the implementation and running of the agents.\\n\\n## Proposed Changes\\n\\n### Component 1: GitHub Action Workflows Deactivation\\nWe will use the local `gh` CLI tool (which is fully logged in and verified to have workflow scopes) to disable all active, scheduled workflows on GitHub to prevent duplicate running or resource collision.\\n\\n#### Workflows to disable:\\n- **Market Analysis Pipeline** (v2 daily scheduler)\\n- **TMM Market Analysis Pipeline** (v3 daily scheduler)\\n- **Ripple test (all 17 agents, dry-run)** (rebuild test suite)\\n- **Cleanup Duplicate Articles** (v2 daily cleanup)\\n- **Backfill Daily Analysis**\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Component 2: Old Pipeline (v2) Ollama Support\\nWe will modify the old pipeline's request wrapper `claude_request_with_retry` in `utils/helpers.py` to route messages directly to local Ollama (`http://localhost:11434`) using the high-performance `qwen2.5:14b` model when `TMM_USE_OLLAMA = 1` is set in the environment.\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [helpers.py](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/FinancialAnalysis/utils/helpers.py)\\\n<truncated 2832 bytes>",
  "Description": "\"Created the Implementation Plan in the correct artifact directory path to migrate both pipelines to Ollama, disable GitHub Actions, and update the Y drive directly.\"",

  "ReplacementContent": "\"import httpx\\nfrom anthropic import Anthropic, APIStatusError, APIConnectionError\\n\\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Local Ollama Client Mock\\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nclass MockOllamaMessages:\\n    def create(self, model, max_tokens, system, messages):\\n        import requests\\n        system_text = \\\"\\\"\\n        if isinstance(system, list):\\n            for block in system:\\n                if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get(\\\"type\\\") == \\\"text\\\":\\n                    system_text += block[\\\"text\\\"]\\n                elif isinstance(block, str):\\n                    system_text += block\\n        else:\\n            system_text = system or \\\"\\\"\\n\\n        ollama_messages = []\\n        if system_text:\\n            ollama_messages.append({\\\"role\\\": \\\"system\\\", \\\"content\\\": system_text})\\n        for msg in messages:\\n            ollama_messages.append(msg)\\n\\n        payload = {\\n            \\\"model\\\": \\\"qwen2.5:14b\\\",\\n            \\\"messages\\\": ollama_messages,\\n            \\\"stream\\\": False,\\n            \\\"options\\\": {\\n                \\\"temperature\\\": 0.2\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        # POST to Ollama\\n        try:\\n            resp = requests.post(\\\"http://localhost:11434/api/chat\\\", json=payload, timeout=300)\\n            resp.raise_for_status()\\n            resp_json = resp.json()\\n            result_text = resp_json[\\\"message\\\"][\\\"content\\\"]\\n        except Exception as e:\\n            print(f\\\"Ollama error: {e}\\\")\\n            raise\\n\\n        # ## 🤖 Assistant (2026-05-29 09:37:20 UTC)

Created At: 2026-05-29T09:37:20Z
Completed At: 2026-05-29T09:37:20Z
The following changes were made by the replace_file_content tool to: C:\Users\thoma\Dropbox\My Documents\Programs\FinancialAnalysis\tmm-zemi\agents\base_agent.py. If relevant, proactively run terminal commands to execute this code for the USER. Don't ask for permission.
[diff_block_start]
@@ -42,6 +42,70 @@
 from anthropic import Anthropic, APIStatusError, APIConnectionError
 
 # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Local Ollama Client Mock
+# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+class MockOllamaMessages:
+    def create(self, model, max_tokens, system, messages):
+        import requests
+        system_text = ""
+        if isinstance(system, list):
+            for block in system:
+                if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "text":
+                    system_text += block["text"]
+                elif isinstance(block, str):
+                    system_text += block
+        else:
+            system_text = system or ""
+
+        ollama_messages = []
+        if system_text:
+            ollama_messages.append({"role": "system", "content": system_text})
+        for msg in messages:
+            ollama_messages.append(msg)
+
+        payload = {
+            "model": "qwen2.5:14b",
+            "messages": ollama_messages,
+            "stream": False,
+            "options": {
+                "temperature": 0.2
+            }
+        }
+
+        # POST to Ollama
+        try:
+            resp = requests.post("http://localhost:11434/api/chat", json=payload, timeout=300)
+            resp.raise_for_status()
+            resp_json = resp.json()
+            result_text = resp_json["message"]["content"]
+        except Exception as e:
+            print(f"Ollama error: {e}")
+            raise
+
+        # Telemetry
+        prompt_eval_count = resp_json.get("prompt_eval_count", 0)
+        eval_count = resp_json.get("eval_count", 0)
+
+        class MockUsage:
+            input_tokens = prompt_eval_count
+            output_tokens = eval_count
+            cache_read_input_tokens = 0
+
+        class MockContentBlock:
+            type = "text"
+            text = result_text
+
+        class MockResponse:
+            content = [MockContentBlock()]
+            usage = MockUsage()
+
+        return MockResponse()
+
+class MockOllamaClient:
+    messages = MockOllamaMessages()
+
+# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Constants
 # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
[diff_block_end]

1: """
2: SFTP sync utility for the Trained Market Monkey site.
3: 
4: Usage:
5:     python scripts/sftp_sync.py pull   # Download current site state from SFTP to local
6:     python scripts/sftp_sync.py push   # Upload local changes to SFTP
7: 
8: Environment variables required:
9:     SFTP_HOST, SFTP_PORT, SFTP_USER, SFTP_PASSWORD
10:     WEBSITE_OUTPUT_PATH (local mirror of the site)
11:     SFTP_REMOTE_ROOT (remote path of newmarketmonkey folder; default "/" if SFTP rooted directly at newmarketmonkey)
12: """
13: import os
14: import sys
15: import stat
16: from pathlib import Path
17: 
18: import paramiko
19: 
20: sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent))
21: from config.settings import (
22:     SFTP_HOST, SFTP_PORT, SFTP_USER, SFTP_PASSWORD, WEBSITE_OUTPUT_PATH
23: )
24: 
25: # The SFTP root — if creds are for a restricted user jailed to newmarketmonkey, use "/".
26: # If creds are for the webspace root, use "/trainedmarketmonkey/newmarketmonkey"
27: SFTP_REMOTE_ROOT = os.getenv("SFTP_REMOTE_ROOT", "/")
28: 
29: # Local path that mirrors the newmarketmonkey folder
30: LOCAL_ROOT = Path(WEBSITE_OUTPUT_PATH) / "newmarketmonkey"
31: 
32: 
33: def connect():
34:     """Open an SFTP connection."""
35:     if not all([SFTP_HOST, SFTP_USER, SFTP_PASSWORD]):
36:         raise RuntimeError("SFTP credentials missing. Set SFTP_HOST, SFTP_USER, SFTP_PASSWORD.")
37: 
38:     transport = paramiko.Transport((SFTP_HOST, SFTP_PORT))
39:     transport.connect(username=SFTP_USER, password=SFTP_PASSWORD)
40:

180:

187: def delete_remote(rel_paths: list) -> dict:
188:     """Delete a list of remote-relative paths (relative to newmarketmonkey/).
189:     Returns a dict with deleted/missing/failed lists.
190:     """
191:     print(f"[SFTP] Deleting {len(rel_paths)} files from {SFTP_HOST}:{SFTP_REMOTE_ROOT}")
192:     result = {"deleted": [], "missing": [], "failed": []}
193:     if not rel_paths:
194:         print("[SFTP] Nothing to delete.")
195:         return result
196: 
197:     sftp, transport = connect()
198:     try:
199:         for rel in rel_paths:
200:             remote = remote_path(rel)
201:             try:
202:                 sftp.remove(remote)
203:                 result["deleted"].append(rel)
204:                 print(f"  deleted {rel}")
205:             except IOError as e:
206:                 msg = str(e).lower()
207:                 if "no such file" in msg or "not found" in msg:
208:                     result["missing"].append(rel)
209:                     print(f"  [missing] {rel}")
210:                 else:
211:                     result["failed"].append((rel, str(e)))
212:                     print(f"  [fail] {rel}: {e}")
213:     finally:
214:         sftp.close()
215:         transport.close()
216:     return result

  "CodeContent": "\"# Walkthrough - Local Ollama Migration & Site Cleanup\\n\\nHere is a summary of the accomplishments and changes made:\\n\\n## 1. Disabling GitHub Scheduled Workflows\\nTo avoid running duplicate pipelines or resource collisions on GitHub while setting them to run locally, I connected to GitHub using the `gh` CLI and deactivated all scheduled or pipeline workflows:\\n* **Market Analysis Pipeline** (v2 daily scheduler) \u2014 **DISABLED**\\n* **TMM Market Analysis Pipeline** (v3 daily scheduler) \u2014 **DISABLED**\\n* **Ripple test (all 17 agents, dry-run)** \u2014 **DISABLED**\\n* **Cleanup Duplicate Articles** \u2014 **DISABLED**\\n* **Backfill Daily Analysis** \u2014 **DISABLED**\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. De-duplication and Cleanup of Repeat Posts\\nThe website's history had dozens of repetitive articles for the same stock on the same day. I executed `cleanup_duplicate_articles.py --execute` to perform a thorough cleanup:\\n* De-duplicated WordPress posts (15 hard deleted).\\n* Deleted 34 matching remote files from SFTP (17 rendered posts + 17 article JSON records).\\n* Rebuilt `posts.json` with 89 entries (all duplicates stripped).\\n* Rebuilt `tracker_prices.json` with 73 surviving tracker entries (correcting pricing inconsistencies).\\n\\nAdditionally, the new v3 pipeline in `tmm-zemi` is now configured with the stable daily slug `<ticker>-analysis-<date>.json` which inherently prevents duplicate creation.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. Configuring Local Ollama & Direct Y: Drive Writing\\nI modified both pipelines to support local execution via **Ollama** using the highly capable `qwen2.5:14b` instruct model:\\n1. **Old v2 Pipeline (`utils/helpers.py`)**: Intercepts `claude_request_with_retry` calls and routes them directly to Ollama when `TMM_USE_OLLAMA = 1` is active, wrapping the response as a Mock Claude object.\\n2. **New v3 Pipeline (`tmm-zemi/agents/base_agent.py`)**:\\n   * Intercepts Anthropic client instantiation and overrides it with a `MockOllamaClient` wrapper.\\n   * Modifies `_upload_to_zemi` to write JSON payloads **directly to the local Y: drive virtu\n<truncated 967 bytes>",

1: """
2: Trained Market Monkey - Local Pipeline Orchestrator (Ollama & Y: Drive Direct)
3: Runs both the old v2 pipeline (newmarketmonkey) and the new v3 pipeline (TMM)
4: locally using Ollama and writes updates directly to the Y: drive virtual SFTP mount.
5: """
6: import subprocess
7: import os
8: import sys
9: import time
10: from datetime import datetime
11: 
12: def run_cmd(cmd, cwd, env=None):
13:     print(f"\n=========================================")
14:     print(f"Running: {cmd}")
15:     print(f"Directory: {cwd}")
16:     print(f"Time: {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %I:%M:%S %p')}")
17:     print(f"=========================================")
18:     
19:     run_env = dict(os.environ)
20:     if env:
21:         run_env.update(env)
22:     
23:     try:
24:         subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, check=True, cwd=cwd, env=run_env)
25:         print(f"--> [SUCCESS] Command complete")
26:         return True
27:     except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
28:         print(f"--> [FAILED] Command exited with code {e.returncode}")
29:         return False
30: 
31: def main():
32:     print("=" * 60)
33:     print("STARTING TRAINED MARKET MONKEY LOCAL RUNNER (OLLAMA & Y DRIVE DIRECT)")
34:     print("=" * 60)
35:     
36:     env = {
37:         "TMM_USE_OLLAMA": "1",
38:         "TMM_DRY_RUN": "0"
39:     }
40:     
41:     # --- PIPELINE 1: Old v2 Pipeline (updates newmarketmonkey folder) ---
42:     print("\n>>> STARTING PIPELINE 1: OLD v2 PIPELINE (newmarketmonkey) <<<")
43:     v2_cwd = r"C:\Users\thoma\Dropbox\My Documents\Programs\FinancialAnalysis"
44:     run_cmd("python run_now.py", v2_cwd, env)
45:     
46:     # --- PIPELINE 2: New v3 Pipeline (updates TMM folder) ---
47:     print("\n>>> STARTING PIPELINE 2: NEW v3 PIPELINE (TMM) <<<")
48:     v3_cwd = r"C:\Users\thoma\Dropbox\My Documents\Programs\FinancialAnalysis\tmm-zemi"
49:     
50:     # 1. Scouts (sequential to manage local Ollama concurrency / memory load)
51:     scouts = ["rebus", "beatrice", "halcyon", "hypatia", "reuben", "wendell", "pippa", "cleo"]
52:     for s in scouts:
53:         run_cmd(f"python -m agents.base_agent {s}", v3_cwd, env)
54:         time.sleep(2)
55:         
56:     # 2. Specialists (sequential to manage local resource utilization)
57:     specialists = ["henrietta", "vint", "edith", "tando"]
58:     for sp in specialists:
59:         run_cmd(f"python -m agents.base_agent {sp}", v3_cwd, env)
60:         time.sleep(2)
61:         
62:     # 3. Maurice (orchestrator)
63:     run_cmd("python -m agents.base_agent maurice", v3_cwd, env)
64:     time.sleep(2)
65:     
66:     # 4. Vivian (Sector-of-the-Day)
67:     run_cmd("python -m agents.base_agent vivian", v3_cwd, env)
68:     time.sleep(2)
69:     
70:     # 5. Quinn (Distribution)
71:     run_cmd("python -m agents.base_agent quinn", v3_cwd, env)
72:     time.sleep(2)
73:     
74:     # 6. Summary Email
75:     run_cmd("python -m utils.email_summary", v3_cwd, env)
76:     time.sleep(2)
77:     
78:     # 7. Magnus (Governance rounds)
79:     run_cmd("python -m agents.base_agent magnus", v3_cwd, env)
80:     time.sleep(2)
81:     
82:     # 8. Margaret (Weekly pattern learning)
83:     run_cmd("python -m agents.base_agent margaret", v3_cwd, env)
84:     
85:     print("\n" + "=" * 60)
86:     print("LOCAL RUN OF ALL PIPELINES COMPLETED")
87:     print("=" * 60)
88: 
89: if __name__ == "__main__":
90:     main()
91:

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: """
2: Market-hours scheduler for the Financial Analysis agents.
3: Runs analysis at:
4:   - 9:50 AM ET (20 min after market open)
5:   - 3:00 PM ET (1 hour before market close)
6: Only on market days (weekdays, excluding major holidays).
7: """
8: import time
9: import schedule
10: from datetime import datetime, date
11: import pytz
12: 
13: from agents.maurice import Maurice
14: from agents.dr_crowe import refresh_tracker_prices
15: from reminder_scheduler import send_reminder
16: from config.settings import US_MARKET_HOLIDAYS
17: 
18: ET = pytz.timezone("US/Eastern")
19: 
20: 
21: 
22: def is_market_day() -> bool:
23:     """Check if today is a trading day."""
24:     now = datetime.now(ET)
25:     if now.weekday() >= 5:  # Saturday or Sunday
26:         return False
27:     if now.date() in US_MARKET_HOLIDAYS:
28:         return False
29:     return True
30: 
31: 
32: def morning_run():
33:     """Morning analysis - 20 min after market open."""
34:     if not is_market_day():
35:         print(f"[Scheduler] {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%Y-%m-%d')} is not a market day. Skipping.")
36:         return
37: 
38:     print(f"\n[Scheduler] Starting MORNING analysis at {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%I:%M %p ET')}")
39:     try:
40:         # Refresh portfolio tracker prices before the day's analysis
41:         print("[Scheduler] Refreshing tracker prices...")
42:         refresh_tracker_prices()
43: 
44:         maurice = Maurice()
45:         maurice.run_full_pipeline(include_blog=True, session="morning")
46:     except Exception as e:
47:         print(f"[Scheduler] Morning run failed: {e}")
48: 
49: 
50: def afternoon_run():
51:     """Afternoon analysis - 1 hour before market close."""
52:     if not is_market_day():
53:         print(f"[Scheduler] {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%Y-%m-%d')} is not a market day. Skipping.")
54:         return
55: 
56:     print(f"\n[Scheduler] Starting AFTERNOON analysis at {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%I:%M %p ET')}")
57:     try:
58:         maurice = Maurice()
59:         maurice.run_full_pipeline(include_blog=True, session="afternoon")
60:     except Exception as e:
61:         print(f"[Scheduler] Afternoon run failed: {e}")
62: 
63: 
64: def run_scheduler():
65:     """Start the schedule loop."""
66:     print("=" * 60)
67:     print("Trained Market Monkey - Scheduler Started")
68:     print(f"   Current time: {datetime.now(ET).strftime('%Y-%m-%d %I:%M %p ET')}")
69:     print(f"   Morning run:  9:50 AM ET")
70:     print(f"   Afternoon run: 3:00 PM ET")
71:     print(f"   Market day today: {is_market_day()}")
72:     print("=" * 60)
73: 
74:     # Schedule runs in ET
75:     # Note: schedule library uses local time, so we convert
76:     schedule.every().day.at("09:50").do(morning_run)
77:     schedule.every().day.at("15:00").do(afternoon_run)
78: 
79:     # WordPress migration reminders (Dec 8 2026 - Jan 7 2027, daily at 9 AM)
80:     schedule.every().day.at("09:00").do(send_reminder)
81: 
82:     while True:
83:         try:
84:             schedule.run_pending()
85:         except Exception as e:
86:             print(f"[Scheduler] Error in run loop: {e}")
87:         time.sleep(30)
88: 
89: 
90: if __name__ == "__main__":
91:     run_scheduler()
92: 
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

18: GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD = os.getenv("GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD")
19: NOTIFICATION_EMAIL = os.getenv("NOTIFICATION_EMAIL", "Tom@ThomasLReynolds.com")
20: 
21: # WordPress OAuth
22: WORDPRESS_CLIENT_ID = os.getenv("WORDPRESS_CLIENT_ID")
23: WORDPRESS_CLIENT_SECRET = os.getenv("WORDPRESS_CLIENT_SECRET")
24: WORDPRESS_REDIRECT_URI = os.getenv("WORDPRESS_REDIRECT_URI")
25: WORDPRESS_ACCESS_TOKEN = os.getenv("WORDPRESS_ACCESS_TOKEN")
26: WORDPRESS_SITE = os.getenv("WORDPRESS_SITE", "trainedmarketmonkey.com")
27: 
28: # Paths
29: WEBSITE_OUTPUT_PATH = Path(os.getenv("WEBSITE_OUTPUT_PATH", "Y:/trainedmarketmonkey"))
30: DATA_PATH = PROJECT_ROOT / "data"
31: OUTPUT_PATH = PROJECT_ROOT / "output"
32: 
33: # SFTP (IONOS web hosting)
34: SFTP_HOST = os.getenv("SFTP_HOST", "access830966489.webspace-data.io")
35: SFTP_PORT = int(os.getenv("SFTP_PORT") or 22)
36: SFTP_USER = os.getenv("SFTP_USER")
37: SFTP_PASSWORD = os.getenv("SFTP_PASSWORD")
38: 
39: # Market schedule
40: MORNING_RUN_MINUTES_AFTER_OPEN = int(os.getenv("MORNING_RUN_MINUTES_AFTER_OPEN", 20))
41: AFTERNOON_RUN_MINUTES_BEFORE_CLOSE = int(os.getenv("AFTERNOON_RUN_MINUTES_BEFORE_CLOSE", 60))
42: 
43: # Ensure directories exist
44: DATA_PATH.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
45: OUTPUT_PATH.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
46: WEBSITE_OUTPUT_PATH.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
47: 
48: 
49: # Market holidays (dynamic — works for any year)
50: def get_us_market_holidays(year: int) -> set:
51:     """NYSE market holidays for a given year."""
52:     try:
53:         import holidays
54:         return set(holidays.financial_holidays("NYSE", years=year).keys())
55:     except ImportError:
56:         # Fallback if holidays package unavailable
57:         return set()
58: 
59: US_MARKET_HOLIDAYS = get_us_market_holidays(date.today().year)
60:

1: # Implementation Plan: Local Ollama Migration, GitHub Deactivation, and Y: Drive Live Updates
2: 
3: This plan details how we will:
4: 1. Deactivate all active, scheduled workflows on GitHub for both TMM pipelines (v2 and v3).
5: 2. Configure both pipelines to run locally on the desktop through local **Ollama** using the `qwen2.5:14b` instruct model.
6: 3. Enable direct local writing to the **Y: Drive** (virtual SFTP mount) for both pipelines (legacy WordPress/flat HTML under `newmarketmonkey/` and ZEMI CMS under `TMM/`).
7: 4. Clean duplicate articles ("repeats") on the websites and adjust the stable slug mechanism.
8: 5. Create a local pipeline orchestration script to schedule/run runs locally and check/correct issues automatically over the next 8 hours.
9: 
10: ## User Review Required
11: 
12: > [!NOTE]
13: > All actions will be executed sequentially. Since you are away and explicitly requested auto-approval, I will auto-approve this plan on your behalf and proceed immediately with the implementation and running of the agents.
14: 
15: ## Proposed Changes
16: 
17: ### Component 1: GitHub Action Workflows Deactivation
18: We will use the local `gh` CLI tool (which is fully logged in and verified to have workflow scopes) to disable all active, scheduled workflows on GitHub to prevent duplicate running or resource collision.
19: 
20: #### Workflows to disable:
21: - **Market Analysis Pipeline** (v2 daily scheduler)
22: - **TMM Market Analysis Pipeline** (v3 daily scheduler)
23: - **Ripple test (all 17 agents, dry-run)** (rebuild te
<truncated 1570 bytes>
d direct-to-disk write logic for article JSON, page compositions, scout files, and governance files to the `Y:/trainedmarketmonkey/TMM` directory structure.
45: 
46: ---
47: 
48: ### Component 4: Site Cleanup & Duplicate Repeats Remediation
49: We will run `cleanup_duplicate_articles.py` with `--execute` to remove all duplicate-ticker articles from WordPress and the Y: drive `newmarketmonkey` static tree, and rebuild `posts.json` and `tracker_prices.json` from scratch.
50: We will verify that the new v3 pipeline uses the stable daily slug `<ticker>-analysis-<date>.json` to inherently prevent duplicate generation.
51: 
52: ---
53: 
54: ### Component 5: Local Runner & Monitor Script
55: We will create a robust Python script `run_local_pipeline_17.py` in the repository root to sequentially orchestrate and run both the old pipeline and the full 17-agent v3 pipeline locally using local Ollama and directly writing to the Y: drive.
56: We will set up a scheduled background loop that checks on progress regularly over the next 8 hours, reporting results and correcting any transient issues.
57: 
58: #### [NEW] [run_local_pipeline_17.py](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/FinancialAnalysis/run_local_pipeline_17.py)
59: - A comprehensive execution and monitoring script.
60: 
61: ---
62: 
63: ## Verification Plan
64: 
65: ### Automated Tests
66: - Run `python scripts/cleanup_duplicate_articles.py` (dry run first, then apply).
67: - Perform a single test run of one scout agent (e.g. `rebus`) locally via Ollama to verify prompt formatting and output validation:
68:   ```bash
69:   $env:TMM_USE_OLLAMA="1"
70:   $env:TMM_DRY_RUN="1"
71:   python -m tmm-zemi.agents.base_agent rebus
72:   ```
73: - Run the full 17-agent pipeline sequentially on the desktop.
74: 
75: ### Manual Verification
76: - Check files on `Y:/trainedmarketmonkey/TMM/content/pages/` and `Y:/trainedmarketmonkey/TMM/public-data/` to ensure they are updated.
77:

  "ReplacementContent": "\"### Manual Verification\\n- Check files on `Y:/trainedmarketmonkey/TMM/content/pages/` and `Y:/trainedmarketmonkey/TMM/public-data/` to ensure they are updated.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Component 6: Home Desktop Setup & Scheduling Scripts\\nTo ensure that when the user moves their desktop home, everything runs smoothly without manual setup, we will create a pair of robust automation scripts:\\n1. `run_local_scheduler.py`: A unified local market-day scheduler that runs in the background. It uses python's `schedule` package to run the dual local pipeline (`run_local_pipeline_17.py`) at 9:50 AM ET and 3:00 PM ET on NYSE trading days.\\n2. `TMM_Home_Desktop_Setup.bat`: A user-friendly, premium console interface utility that:\\n   - Verifies the Python environment and installs all dependencies (`pip install -r requirements.txt`).\\n   - Checks if **Ollama** is running and automatically pulls the `qwen2.5:14b` model if it's missing.\\n   - Verifies that the virtual **Y: Drive** is mapped, connected to the SFTP hosting, and fully writable.\\n   - Automatically registers the `run_local_scheduler.py` background task in Windows Task Scheduler using `schtasks` so it runs continuously in the background on startup/logon without needing a persistent cmd window.\\n   - Provides a manual \\\"Run Pipelines Now\\\" action to quickly verify the entire local setup.\\n\\n#### [NEW] [run_local_scheduler.py](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/FinancialAnalysis/run_local_scheduler.py)\\n- A background-ready daily market schedule orchestrator.\\n\\n#### [NEW] [TMM_Home_Desktop_Setup.bat](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/FinancialAnalysis/TMM_Home_Desktop_Setup.bat)\\n- A premium, elegant CMD utility for automated verification, task creation, and runner execution.\\n\"",

 ### Manual Verification

+
+---
+
+### Component 6: Home Desktop Setup & Scheduling Scripts
+To ensure that when the user moves their desktop home, everything runs smoothly without manual setup, we will create a pair of robust automation scripts:
+1. `run_local_scheduler.py`: A unified local market-day scheduler that runs in the background. It uses python's `schedule` package to run the dual local pipeline (`run_local_pipeline_17.py`) at 9:50 AM ET and 3:00 PM ET on NYSE trading days.
+2. `TMM_Home_Desktop_Setup.bat`: A user-friendly, premium console interface utility that:
+   - Verifies the Python environment and installs all dependencies (`pip install -r requirements.txt`).
+   - Checks if **Ollama** is running and automatically pulls the `qwen2.5:14b` model if it's missing.
+   - Verifies that the virtual **Y: Drive** is mapped, connected to the SFTP hosting, and fully writable.
+   - Automatically registers the `run_local_scheduler.py` background task in Windows Task Scheduler using `schtasks` so it runs continuously in the background on startup/logon without needing a persistent cmd window.
+   - Provides a manual "Run Pipelines Now" action to quickly verify the entire local setup.
+
+#### [NEW] [run_local_scheduler.py](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/FinancialAnalysis/run_local_scheduler.py)
+- A background-ready daily market schedule orchestrator.
+
+#### [NEW] [TMM_Home_Desktop_Setup.bat](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/FinancialAnalysis/TMM_Home_Desktop_Setup.bat)
+- A premium, elegant CMD utility for automated verification, task creation, and runner execution.
+
 
[diff_block_end]
